rulings.Case Title: RUSSELL BRIMER VS. BUSCH ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joint Motion And Joint Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And To Enter Stipulation And [Proposed] Order Re Consent Judgment; Affidavit Of Compliance With 11 Ccr 3000 Et Seq.
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 16, 2007, LINE 2. PLAINTIFF RUSSELL BRIMER AND DEFENDANTS BUSCH ENTERTAINMENT CORPORATION AND SEA WORLD, INC.'S Joint Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And To Enter Stipulation Regarding Consent Judgment. rulings.Case Title: PAUL H. MELBOSTAD VS. DONALD G FISHER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn In Support Of Mtn For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 16, 2007, LINE 6. DEFENDANTS DONALD FISHER, JAMES SUTTON AND KEVIN HENEGHAN'S MOTION For Protective Order IS OFF CALENDAR. DUPLICATE ENTRY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL H. MELBOSTAD VS. DONALD G FISHER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Re-Notice Of Motion And Motion In Supporet Of Defts' Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 16, 2007, LINE 6. DEFENDANTS DONALD FISHER, JAMES SUTTON AND KEVIN HENEGHAN'S Re-Notice Of Motion For Protective Order IS GRANTED FOR THE REASONS SET FORTH IN DEFENDANT'S PROPOSED ORDER WITH ONE ADDITIONAL OBSERVATION. THE ACTUAL TIME RECORDED IS RELEVANT TO BUT NOT DETERMINATIVE OF A REASONABLE FEE AWARD. AT THIS POINT, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO DEMONSTRATE THAT A DEPOSITION ON THIS SUBJECT IS NECESSARY TO ADDRESS THE MERITS OF THE FEE MOTION. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: NEIL ADAMS VS. CORT BUSINESS SERVICES CORPORATION et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 16, 2007, LINE 3. DEFENDANTS CORT BUSINESS SERVICES CORPORATION, DAWN KLIPPEL AND KEVIN DANA'S DEMURRER TO THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO MAY 22, 2007 ON COURT'S OWN MOTION. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: TRACEY MCCARROLL VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 16, 2007, LINE 4. DEFENDANTS PAR ELECTRIC CONTRACTORS, INC., KENNETH BRUCE, MATTHEW CRIST AND JARED JOHNSON'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. GRANTED AS TO PARAGRAPH 19 BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS ONLY ALLEGED ECONOMIC HARM. GRANTED AS TO PARAGRAPH 31 AS TO P.G. & E., FAILURE TO MEET REQUIREMENTS OF CIVIL CODE SECTION 3294 (B). OVERRULED AS TO INDIVIDUALS. GRANTED AS TO PARAGRAPH 38; FAILURE TO PLEAD FACTS GIVING RISE TO AN EGREGIOUS BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM. LEAVE TO AMEND WITHIN TWENTY DAYS IS GRANTED. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Case Title: AS YOU SOW VS. INDUSTRIAL BOLT & SUPPLY INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing On Defendant Industrial Bolt & Supply, Inc.'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 16, 2007, LINE 10. DEFENDANT INDUSTRIAL BOLT & SUPPLY, INC.'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION IS GRANTED. THE EVIDENCE FAILS TO ESTABLISH SUFFICIENT FACTS FOR THE EXERCISE OF GENERAL JURISDICTION. SUFFICIENT FACTS HAVE BEEN ALLEGED TO ESTABLISH A BASIS FOR LIMITED JURISDICTION. HOWEVER, TO EXERCISE SUCH JURISDICTION IN THIS CASE OFFENDS THE NOTION OF FAIR PLAY AND SUBSTANTIAL JUSTICE. =(302/PJM/AP)

rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 16, 2007, LINE 5. DEFENDANT AMERICAN CONTRACTORS INDEMNITY COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON WEDNESDAY, MAY 16, 2007, LINE 8. DEFENDANT JERIC ALEGRE'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons IS DENIED. THE COMPLAINT SERVED UPON DEFENDANT CONFORMS TO THE COMPLAINT FILED. DEFENDANT HAS FIVE DAYS TO RESPOND OR, IF SERVED WITH AN AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHIN FIVE DAYS, THE TIME TO RESPOND WILL BE GOVERNED BY THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. =(302/PJM)
